task_yaml.task:
Newsletter deadline is tomorrow and I need a year-in-review digest
for the Flask project. The repo tarball is at
/tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/flask_repo.tar.gz — use the git-history
and Markdown formatting skills to crunch the commit history and produce a
clean writeup. Write /tmp_workspace/results/digest.md as a Markdown
summary I can paste straight into our team newsletter. I want it to read
end-to-end so I get a feel for what shipped, what got fixed, what got
removed or deprecated, where performance moved, and who was actually
pushing things forward over the year. Aim for around 5 short bullets plus
a small contributor table at the bottom with login, commit count, and
rough areas touched. Keep it tight, fact-based, and grounded in real
commits in the tarball — don't make stuff up if a category genuinely
had no activity.

Use the last commit's commit-date as "today" and look back 365 days.
State the explicit window date-range somewhere in the digest. Each
bullet should give a 1-line description plus 2 short commit SHAs of
7 hex chars. The contributor table should cover every contributor
who made at least 1 commit in the 1-year window, one row per
contributor, sorted by commit_count descending.

One more thing I really care about: somewhere in the digest, call
out the single largest-scale code change in the year — by which I
mean the commit that touched the most files, with total lines
changed as a tiebreak. Give me its short SHA explicitly so I can go
read it. Anchor every bullet to a concrete theme visible in the
cited commits, not a generic "maintenance work" summary.
